author: racytech
content: Thanks <@606464909907197983>! This is a great tool. I wish I could add artex there, so I can have a clearer picture even tho I know it should perform well enough. Do I have to develop a full EL client for this? <@412614104222531604>  <@199561711278227457> or is there a minimum requirements?

author: samcm
content: We use the engine API, so if you're compliant with that it should work. But to appear on The Lab we'd need to host the instance on our cluster with the same reference hardware as everyone else but we're out of instances 😅

author: racytech
content: Sure, on the same reference hardware... so basically what I need is engine API + full sync support + 3 instances. What hardware do you guys use? I see Intel Core i7-13700H is that a laptop or a mini pc, intel NUC?

author: samcm
content:
Yeah pretty much, it’s a nuc 😊

Exact specs are at the top if you want an exact comparison. Being truthful though we would probably need the client to go through the normal mainnet procedure before we hosted in/ran on the Lab (not sure on the specifics but I think we usually depend on an audit or something along those terms. Cc <@412614104222531604> ?)

author: racytech
content: normal mainnet procedure - meaning that it should be outhere running? or is it full re-execution from genesis?

author: samcm
content: Sorry thats poor wording on my side, i mean for the client to be picked up in our testing stack and processes. Syncing mainnet would be minimum, as well as audits, and hardening etc. There's others but i'm entirely sure of the specifics

author: samcm
content: Syncing mainnet from genesis would definitely be one of them 🙂

author: racytech
content: Fair enough. Is there a checklist for a new clients who wants to join the network? I am sure I just need a wrapper around artex and a client should be ready

author: racytech
content: Double checked reference hardware... not sure if artex will show the same numbers... had no chance to run on similar
